title: Arctanthemum arcticum
taxonomy: Arctanthemum arcticum is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It belongs to the genus Arctanthemum, which is a group of plants closely related to the genus Anthemis. The species name arcticum indicates its typical habitat in Arctic regions. Arctanthemum arcticum is also known as Arctic chamomile or Arctic marguerite. This classification is based on the plant's morphological characteristics and phylogenetic relationships.
uses: Arctanthemum arcticum is known to have traditional medicinal uses, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness. It is traditionally used to treat various health issues, including gastrointestinal problems, fever, and inflammation. The plant's properties are thought to have antibacterial, antifungal, and anti-inflammatory effects. Arctanthemum arcticum is sometimes used to treat respiratory issues, such as coughs and colds, due to its expectorant properties. However, the safety and efficacy of using Arctanthemum arcticum for medicinal purposes should be evaluated by a healthcare professional.

parts: Arctanthemum arcticum, also known as Arctic oat or Arctic chamomile, is a plant native to the Arctic tundra. The medicinal parts of this plant are primarily its leaves and flowers. intro: Arctanthemum arcticum, also known as Arctic Chamomile or Arctic Daisy, is a perennial flowering plant native to the Arctic regions. This plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including insomnia, anxiety, and digestive problems, due to its calming and soothing properties. The bioactive constituents of Arctanthemum arcticum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. The most common herbal preparations made with Arctanthemum arcticum include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to promote relaxation and improve sleep quality.
